blocks.content: In front of the Military Community Gate.

blocks.speaker: Guard 1
blocks.content: Heh, they're all scared. What should we do with them?

blocks.speaker: Guard 2
blocks.content: Kill them? Or maybe take them alive?
The guards' voices were thin and raspy, as if their vocal cords were strained.
But more surprising was the aura they emitted.
When facing a creature with murderous intent, whether it be a knife, fangs, or claws, one feels a strong presence, but each has a different nature.
A murderer? A beast?
The aura from these guards was entirely different.
An unknown creature. Or perhaps a complex mix of existing creatures.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: 'I can't be humiliated again.'
Kuan was ready to strike at any moment.
If they revealed their true nature, he would shoot external force in three directions.
First, he would cut down the ones on the left, then use the recoil to accelerate and swiftly eliminate the rest.
Tick. Tick. Tick.
A faint sound, barely noticeable to anyone but a Schema user, reached Kuan's ears.
It was the sound of claws extending from the right hand of the guard facing Armin.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: Everyone, stop!
A loud voice boomed from beyond the gate.
Kuan immediately grounded himself and neutralized the external force, but his sword had already tasted blood.
Thud! Thud!
The heads of two guards on the left fell to the ground.
Siina shuddered as she realized that Kuan, who had been standing beside her moments ago, was now among the enemy ranks.
She hadn't noticed when he moved or acted.
While most swordsmen use propulsion for surprise attacks, Kuan moved by suction.
An instant acceleration with no preparation or warm-up needed.
She felt fortunate that Kuan wasn't an enemy.

blocks.speaker: Guard 3
blocks.content: You bastard! I'll kill you!
As the guards charged at Kuan, he raised his sword horizontally, ready to fight.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: Stop, you idiots! Didn't you hear the boss?
The guards froze in place.
Their faces, though not yet transformed, were twisted into monstrous expressions.
With a clanking sound, the iron gate opened, and a large middle-aged man stepped out.
He wore a coat with long, soft fur like mink, and his shoulders were adorned with the taxidermied heads of terrified weasels.
His unkempt hair hung to his shoulders, and he wore dark glasses with pointed lenses.
This was Frankwine, the leader of Yamaeng, who controlled all the hunting grounds near the mainland.
Armin walked up to him and asked, "Are you the person in charge here?"

blocks.speaker: Frankwine
blocks.content: Hmm.
Frankwine stroked his beard and examined Armin, then grinned, revealing gold teeth.

blocks.speaker: Frankwine
blocks.content: Young and full of energy. Yes, I'm the boss here. What do you want?

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: We have a request for the military community.

blocks.speaker: Frankwine
blocks.content: Ah! So you want to do business. Business is essential. Come in, come in.

blocks.speaker: Guard 4
blocks.content: Boss! They killed two of our men! Are you just going to let them in?
Frankwine glanced at the two headless bodies and shrugged.

blocks.speaker: Frankwine
blocks.content: So what? Men fight, and sometimes they die, or get their heads cut off. What's the big deal?

blocks.speaker: Guard 4
blocks.content: But boss…
Frankwine's face twisted in annoyance.

blocks.speaker: Frankwine
blocks.content: Are you going to keep arguing with me? Are you dead? No, you're alive, right? Then stop worrying about others and do your job.
The guards glared at Armin's group but ultimately had to step aside.
Violent and absolute. That was Armin's first impression of Yamaeng.
As they walked to the office, Frankwine briefly introduced the factories.
Seeing it in person and hearing about it confirmed the immense scale.

They produced food made from Elixirs, weapons, hunting supplies, medicines, and more. The vast amount of Elixirs obtained from renting out nearby hunting grounds made it the largest community on the mainland.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: 'They're definitely different from anyone we've met before.'
Feeling cautious, Armin probed gently.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: Thank you for your time. I heard you were busy.

blocks.speaker: Frankwine
blocks.content: Ah, I drank too much yesterday. Didn't feel great. But what's the point of resting? Might as well do something useful, haha.
Frankwine proudly opened the office door, like a hotel manager showing off a room.
It was a cramped, filthy room, reeking of cigarettes and alcohol.

blocks.speaker: Frankwine
blocks.content: Wait here for a moment. I'll finish up and be right back.
After leading Armin's group into the office, Frankwine turned and entered a room at the end of the hallway.
A handsome blond man with slicked-back hair was lounging with his legs on the desk. He jumped up in surprise.

blocks.speaker: Meatgun
blocks.content: Boss, you're here.

blocks.speaker: Frankwine
blocks.content: I told you, if you sit at my desk again, I'll kill you.
Meatgun flew like a cannonball, crashing into the wall. As he fell to the floor, a huge crack appeared in the wall.
As Meatgun clutched his forehead and stood up, Frankwine opened the door and said, "Sit at my desk again, and I'll kill you."
Thud.
The door closed.
Armin's group sat in the office, all frowning.
The stench of alcohol and cigarettes was mixed with another foul odor.

blocks.speaker: Siina
blocks.content: What is that smell?
The men had an idea but remained silent.

blocks.speaker: Etella
blocks.content: It smells like fish…
Armin quickly changed the subject.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: Isn't it strange that the head of the military community is suddenly so friendly?

blocks.speaker: Siina
blocks.content: We can't let our guard down. The guards were odd, and Frankwine's aura was really strange. Something might be happening that we don't know about…
Etella and Kuan nodded.
But even if something was happening elsewhere, they couldn't know.
Frankwine entered with a cheerful smile.

blocks.speaker: Frankwine
blocks.content: Sorry about that. Got a bit behind.

blocks.speaker: Siina
blocks.content: Why is this office so dirty? And what's that smell? It's giving me a headache.
Frankwine looked at her as if wondering why she was asking, then burst into laughter.

blocks.speaker: Frankwine
blocks.content: Haha! Right! I had a wild night yesterday…
Armin gestured for him to sit.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: Please, have a seat. We have something important to discuss.
Frankwine sat down, looking sheepish but calculating quickly.
All four were highly skilled, and there were two innocent-looking young women among them.
Such people were rare in purgatory, where survival was everything.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: 'They're from another world.'
That made things easier. Frankwine wasn't from purgatory either.

blocks.speaker: Frankwine
blocks.content: So, what brings such formidable people to this shabby place?
He hadn't forgotten his subordinates' deaths.
But Frankwine's feelings were more businesslike than mournful.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: We want to rent your factory.

blocks.speaker: Frankwine
blocks.content: Oh, starting strong. What do you want to make?

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: The goal was to produce a large Tagis.

blocks.speaker: Frankwine
blocks.content: Oh, starting strong. What do you want to make?
Armin provided only the necessary information.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: They would supply the Elixirs and materials and pay for the factory rental with Elixirs.

blocks.speaker: Frankwine
blocks.content: Oh, starting strong. What do you want to make?
Frankwine licked his lips.
He didn't dislike the offer. Every deal was golden to a businessman.
But something felt off.

blocks.speaker: Frankwine
blocks.content: 'Something's happening with the rebels.'

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: We'll make sure you're well compensated.

blocks.speaker: Frankwine
blocks.content: Of course, you should come to us for such a big job. But Elixirs alone won't do. We're doing too well lately.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: Then what?
Elixirs were the most common currency for obtaining things on the mainland.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: Armin didn't understand why Elixirs wouldn't suffice.

blocks.speaker: Frankwine
blocks.content: Hmm, what to do.
Frankwine pretended to ponder, then scribbled something at his desk.
He tore a page from his memo book, crumpled it, and tossed it on the table.
Armin slowly unfolded it and read the contents.
His eyes, covered by a bandage, glowed.
Hunting List
Photosynthetic Behemoth Olkir – Hunting Rank: A
Bat Demon Izeremong – Hunting Rank: A
Invisible Spirit Hororos – Hunting Rank: B
True Demon-Type Galtomic – Hunting Rank: S
Infinite Cell Replicant Kenser – Hunting Rank: S
Devourer Spirit Kouzen – Hunting Rank: A
Acid Venom King Muusa – Hunting Rank: B
Armored Mineral-Type Ringer – Hunting Rank: SS
